'''Matty Dawson-Jones''' is an English former [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|wg}}er or {{rlp|ce}} for [[Hunslet RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

He has previously played for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]], spending time on loan from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] at [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], and spent time on loan from the [[St Helens R.F.C.|Saints]] at the [[Rochdale Hornets]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He also played for the [[Leigh Centurions]] and [[Hull F.C.]] in the Super League and [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Matthew Dawson was born in [[Pontefract]], [[West Yorkshire, England]].

==Career==
===Huddersfield Giants===
Dawson, a [[Castleford Tigers]] player at youth level, made his senior début in the [[Super League]] with the [[Huddersfield Giants]]. Whilst at Huddersfield, Dawson also played for [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] on loan in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]], before signing for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], and making 50 appearances.

He has played for the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and has played a few times for the [[Rochdale Hornets]] on dual registration.

===Hull FC===
In 2018, Dawson-Jones signed a contract with [[Hull FC]] which will see him play with the club from 2019.

In January 2020, Dawson-Jones signed a one-year deal with [[RFL Championship]] side [[Bradford Bulls]]. Following the cancelled season he took up the option of an extra year to stay for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18688608.dawson-jones-takes-easy-decision-stay-bulls-2021/|title=Dawson-Jones takes "easy" decision to stay at Bulls in 2021 |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=1 September 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref>

===Hunslet RLFC===
On 17 November 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Hunslet RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=17 Nov 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Former St Helens and Leigh star seals cross-Championship switch ahead of 2026|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/matty-dawson-jones-joins-hunslet-ahead-of-2026}}</ref>

He announced his retirement on 28 January 2026, after just 2 games for [[Hunslet RLFC]].
